issues.description: SEED.md explicitly requires a RootSpec methodology diagram: 'if possible, create an image that depicts the RootSpec methodology in a simple way: a spec surrounding the development cycle and only allowing valid solutions to pass through. If an image is not possible, create a diagram using SVG.' No SVG, no image, and no diagram of any kind exists in the source. The How It Works section uses only text cards.
issues.expected: A visual diagram (SVG or image) depicting the RootSpec methodology — spec surrounding the development cycle, showing only valid solutions passing through
issues.actual: No diagram present. The How It Works section shows four text cards (/rs-init, /rs-spec, /rs-impl, /rs-validate) with no accompanying visual
issues.suggestion: Add an inline <svg> element to the How It Works section or as a standalone section, depicting the five-level spec hierarchy or the spec-as-validation-gate concept

issues.description: SEED.md specifies the hierarchy explorer should show 'Visual connections show which levels each can reference (arrows flow upward only). Hovering a level highlights its allowed references.' The implementation is a plain accordion with expand/collapse chevrons (▼/▲) only — no upward-flow arrows, no inter-level connector lines, no hover reference highlighting.
issues.expected: Visual arrows showing upward-only reference flow between levels, and hover states that highlight which levels are referenceable from the hovered level
issues.actual: A plain accordion with ▼/▲ chevrons. Clicking expands text content. No arrows, no hover-based reference highlighting, no inter-level visual connections
issues.suggestion: Add CSS arrows or connector lines between level cards, and a hover interaction that dims non-referenceable levels (e.g. hovering L3 dims L4 and L5 since they cannot be referenced upward)

issues.description: Meta banner links point to https://github.com/rootspec/demos/blob/main/greenfield/SEED.md and https://github.com/rootspec/demos/tree/main/greenfield/rootspec. The CTA GitHub link points to https://github.com/rootspec/rootspec. SEED.md says to link to the GitHub repo but does not specify the exact URL. These URLs cannot be verified without network access and could be hallucinated paths.
issues.expected: Links should point to confirmed, real GitHub URLs for this project's repo
issues.actual: Uses assumed org 'rootspec' with repo 'demos' for spec/seed links and repo 'rootspec' for the CTA. The org 'rootspec' is internally consistent with the CLAUDE.md context describing 'rootspec/demos' as the demos repo, but cannot be verified
issues.suggestion: Confirm https://github.com/rootspec/demos and https://github.com/rootspec/rootspec are live repos; update URLs if the actual GitHub location differs

issues.description: The theme toggle renders '◗ Dark' when in dark mode (showing the option to switch to dark, i.e. currently in light mode). The '◗' character (U+25D7, right-half black circle) is not a recognizable dark-mode or moon icon. In contrast '☀ Light' (sun) is immediately recognizable. This creates visual inconsistency in iconography.
issues.expected: A recognizable dark-mode symbol pairing the ☀ sun icon — typically a moon/crescent (🌙) or similar well-understood icon
issues.actual: '◗ Dark' — uses a geometric half-circle that does not conventionally represent nighttime or dark mode
issues.suggestion: Replace '◗' with '🌙' or '●' or drop the icon and use plain text 'Dark' / 'Light' labels with consistent treatment

issues.description: Two distinct anchor elements share data-test='github-link': one in the hero section (line 42) and one in the CTA section (line 135). Duplicate data-test values make automated test selectors ambiguous — Cypress will match the first occurrence, which is the hero link, not the intended CTA link tested by US-005.
issues.expected: Unique data-test attributes per element so test selectors unambiguously target the intended element
issues.actual: data-test='github-link' on both line 42 (hero 'GitHub →' button) and line 135 (CTA '★ Star on GitHub' link)
issues.suggestion: Rename the hero link to data-test='hero-github-link'; keep data-test='github-link' on the CTA link which is what US-005 tests

issues.description: The 'After' button in the Before/After toggle has no data-test attribute. The 'Before' button has data-test='before-after-toggle' but the sibling 'After' button is untagged. This inconsistency makes the After button untargetable by data-test selectors in future tests.
issues.expected: Both Before and After buttons should have data-test attributes for consistent and maintainable test targeting
issues.actual: Only the 'Before' button has data-test='before-after-toggle'; the 'After' button (line 73) has no data-test
issues.suggestion: Add data-test='before-after-toggle-after' to the After button, and consider renaming the Before button's selector to data-test='before-after-toggle-before' for symmetry

issues.description: SEED.md says the meta banner should be 'persistent, visible'. The banner is in normal document flow above the sticky header — it scrolls out of view when the user scrolls down the page. The sticky header only includes the nav bar (logo + theme toggle), not the meta banner.
issues.expected: Meta banner should remain visible (persistent) as specified in SEED.md: 'A persistent, visible banner'
issues.actual: Banner scrolls out of view on scroll; only the nav bar is sticky
issues.suggestion: Consider making the meta banner sticky by adding it inside or above the sticky header, or accept this as a known limitation and document it

qualityScore.notes: content: -5 for unverifiable GitHub URLs (REV-003). visual: -5 for missing methodology diagram visual (REV-001). alignment: -5 for missing SEED.md-specified diagram (REV-001 spec requirement). accessibility: -2 for unclear dark icon (REV-004). polish: -5 for duplicate data-test='github-link' across hero and CTA (REV-005). Overall strong implementation with key content requirements met.
